Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 And 583.250
Off Calendar Sep-05-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
SCOTT H. LEVY, Esq., INFINITY LAW GROUP LLP (925) 732-1188 slevy@infinitylawca.com
Attorneys for Plaintiff, Jessica Bogac-Moore
EDWARD J. VALDESPINO, Esq., GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (949) 255-6981 evaldespino@grsm.com, Attorneys for Defendant LYFT, INCfor Defendant.

Defendant seeks clarification as to the Court's tentative ruling regarding setting new hearing date for this motion and submission of moving papers to the Court. Court provides clarification.

Hearing is held. Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.210 And 583.250.

Ordered off calendar to enable plaintiff's counsel to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies) and to file an admissible declaration "under the laws of the state of California." (See Kulshrestha v. First Union Commercial Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, 612.) Counsel may set a new hearing date on a Mon.-Thur. after September 19. The papers shall bear the new hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
